title: Eaton Corporation (ETN) — Intrinsic Value & Prospect Score
description: We ran a full discounted cash flow on Eaton Corporation using FY2025 actuals. One number. Every assumption disclosed. Here's what ETN is really worth. Economic Prospect Score: 86/100 — Strong Prospect.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale:
" data-astro-cid-s37fh3z2>
Eaton is perfectly positioned for the massive infrastructure and electrification megatrends. Demand from data centers, utility grid upgrades, and reshoring of manufacturing provides visibility into sustained above-average growth, justifying a robust 12% FCF CAGR over the next half-decade.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale:
" data-astro-cid-s37fh3z2>
With the 10Y Treasury at 4.18%, a 9% discount rate implies an equity risk premium of roughly 4.8%, appropriate for a mature, highly profitable industrial company. Eaton operates with stable cash flows and manageable leverage, warranting a lower hurdle rate than highly cyclical or speculative peers.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale:
" data-astro-cid-s37fh3z2>
A 3.0% perpetuity growth rate assumes Eaton will match long-term nominal global economic growth. Power management and infrastructure are fundamental, enduring needs, ensuring the company remains relevant indefinitely without assuming unrealistic permanent market share gains.

verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 18, 2026).

faq.question: Why use a 9% discount rate for Eaton?
faq.answer: Westmount Research. "Eaton Corporation (ETN) Intrinsic Value: A DCF Analysis." westmountfundamentals.com, March 18, 2026.

prospectVerdictDetail: Eaton Corporation is exceptionally well-positioned at the intersection of powerful secular mega-trends: electrification, grid modernization, and the massive buildout of AI data center infrastructure. The company commands a dominant market share in mission-critical electrical components and power management systems. With accelerating revenue growth and expanding margins driven by these once-in-a-generation capital investments, Eaton's economic moat is both widening and deepening.

competitiveMomentum.title: Competitive Momentum
competitiveMomentum.summary: Eaton's competitive momentum is robust, fueled by unprecedented demand for power management solutions across utility, industrial, and data center markets, driving sustained double-digit top-line acceleration.

factors.name: Revenue Growth vs. Peers
factors.rationale: Eaton's top-line growth significantly outpaces broader industrial averages, propelled by the urgent need for expanded electrical infrastructure to support AI computing and grid resilience.

factors.name: Market Share Trajectory
factors.rationale: The company maintains leading positions in electrical equipment and continues to gain share through strategic partnerships, such as its recent collaboration with NVIDIA to power next-generation AI platforms.

factors.name: Pricing Power
factors.rationale: Strong demand dynamics and constrained supply chains in critical electrical components afford Eaton substantial pricing power, allowing it to drive margin expansion.

factors.name: Product Velocity
factors.rationale: Eaton is rapidly innovating in high-growth areas, evident in its acquisition of Boyd Thermal for liquid-cooling solutions, strengthening its integrated 'grid-to-chip' offering.

moatDurability.title: Moat Durability
moatDurability.summary: Eaton's wide economic moat is fortified by high switching costs, an extensive installed base, and critical technical expertise required for complex, highly regulated power systems.

factors.name: Switching Costs
factors.rationale: Eaton's electrical systems are deeply embedded into critical infrastructure—from hospitals to hyper-scale data centers. The risks and costs of switching out mission-critical power management architecture are prohibitively high.

factors.name: Network Effects
factors.rationale: While traditional network effects are limited, its massive installed base creates a powerful recurring revenue stream for aftermarket services, software, and maintenance, cementing customer stickiness.

factors.name: Regulatory & IP Position
factors.rationale: Power management is highly regulated, requiring stringent safety certifications and deep engineering expertise. Eaton's vast IP portfolio and established relationships with utilities and regulators create significant barriers to entry.

factors.name: Capital Intensity Advantage
factors.rationale: Eaton operates an efficient manufacturing footprint, enabling it to scale production to meet surging demand while generating robust free cash flow, translating to high returns on invested capital.

sentimentCatalyst.title: Sentiment & Catalysts
sentimentCatalyst.summary: Market sentiment surrounding Eaton is overwhelmingly positive, driven by its status as a premier 'picks and shovels' play on the AI revolution and the broader electrification of the global economy.

factors.name: Earnings Estimate Revisions
factors.rationale: Analysts consistently revise estimates upward as the scale and duration of the data center buildout (estimated at nearly $7 trillion) and grid modernization efforts become clearer.

factors.name: News & Narrative Sentiment
factors.rationale: The narrative is incredibly strong. News highlighting partnerships with NVIDIA and acquisitions in liquid cooling position Eaton centrally within the most powerful growth themes in the market.

factors.name: Management & Capital Allocation
factors.rationale: Management executes flawlessly, utilizing strong cash flows to fund organic growth, pursue strategic, accretive acquisitions (like Boyd Thermal), and return capital to shareholders via consistent dividend increases.

prospectKeyRisks: A sharper-than-expected macroeconomic slowdown could delay or scale back planned capital expenditures in industrial and commercial construction.
Supply chain bottlenecks for critical raw materials (e.g., copper) or electronic components could constrain Eaton's ability to meet surging demand.
The valuation is priced for perfection; any moderation in the current explosive growth expectations for AI infrastructure could trigger a multiple contraction.
prospectKeyCatalysts: Accelerated deployment of generative AI models driving a sustained, multi-year supercycle in hyper-scale data center construction and power density upgrades.
Massive government funding (e.g., Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act) flowing into grid modernization and renewable energy integration.
The electrification of everything—from commercial vehicle fleets to residential heating (via partnerships like SPAN)—driving secular volume growth.
prospectMethodology: Consensus Analysis — Economic Prospect Score averaging independent evaluations from Opus 4.6 and Gemini 3.1. Gemini scored ETN at 88/100 and Opus at 83/100. Each factor score is the arithmetic mean of both models. Three pillars: Competitive Momentum (0-35), Moat Durability (0-35), and Sentiment & Catalysts (0-30).
